chunks.text: Page 1 THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry & Mr. Justice Jawad Akbar Sarwana. C.P. No. D – 3822 of 2024 [Azeem Ahmed v. Kaleem Ahmed and others] Petitioner: Azeem Ahmed son of Noor Muhammad through Mr. W.R. Sheikh, Advocate, a/w Ms. Afshan Rani, Advocate. Date of hearing: 29-08-2024 Date of order: 29-08-2024 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. – 1] Urgency granted. 3] Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 2&4] This constitution petition emanates from an order dated 06.03.2023 passed by the Senior Civil Judge in Suit No. 225/2019, dismissing the Petitioner’s/Plaintiff’s application under section 151 CPC which prayed appointment of a Commissioner to take accounts and make inquiries into the assets of the school running on the suit property in a suit filed for its accounts. Civil Revision No. 57/2023 against that order too was dismissed by order dated 31.01.2024. The orders impugned are essentially to the effect that where the Petitioner/Plaintiff has yet to lead evidence on the issues settled in the suit to prove that he has a case for accounts, the appointment of a Commissioner to take accounts would be premature. Learned counsel for the Petitioner is unable to convince us that such orders are perverse or that those suffer from any jurisdictional defect. Therefore, there is no reason for interference in writ jurisdiction. The petition is dismissed in limine with a cost of Rs.10,000/- which shall be deposited by the Petitioner with the Nazir of the Court in the account of the Medical Clinic of the High Court. JUDGE JUDGE SHABAN*
